name: Phase-Resolved Operator (General)
equation: ⧒f(z_k) = f(|z_k|, θ_R,k),  θ_R,k = θ_R,k-1 + clip(wrap_pi(θ_k - θ_k-1), -π_a,k-1, π_a,k-1)
description: General definition of a Phase-Resolved Operator (PRO): evaluate analytic functions on the lifted phase cover (θ_R) of a complex signal to eliminate branch-cut discontinuities. Applies to ⧒log, ⧒sqrt, ⧒pow and other analytic functions.
assumptions: θ_k = Arg(z_k) is the principal branch
evidence: PRO outputs are single-valued and continuous on the lifted cover
Branch fault rate reduces versus principal branch for log/sqrt/pow functions
